title: Owen Omogiafo Joins APGC Board of Trustees
description: The President/Chief Executive Officer of Transnational Corporation of Nigeria Plc (Transcorp), Mrs. Owen Omogiafo has join the board of trustee of the Association of Power Generation Company (APGC). S
html:
The President/Chief Executive Officer of Transnational Corporation of Nigeria Plc (Transcorp), Mrs. Owen Omogiafo has join the board of trustee of the Association of Power Generation Company (APGC).

She replaces Mr. Emmanuel Nnorom who represented Transcorp Power Limited on the Board of Trustees of the association and retired after serving successfully for four year.

Owen Omogiafo is coming with a wealth of experience,  prior to joining Transcorp,  she was the MD/CEO of Transcorp Hotels Plc and Executive Director of Transcorp. She has over two decades of corporate experience in organisational development, human capital management, banking, change management and hospitality.

Owen holds a B.Sc. in Sociology & Anthropology from the University of Benin, an M.Sc. in Human Resource Management from the London School of Economics and Political Science and is an alumnus of the Lagos Business School and IESE Business School, Spain. Owen is also a member of the Chartered Institute of Personnel and Development, UK and a Certified Change Manager with the Prosci Institute, USA.. She has received multiple awards and named as one of Africa’s top 100 Female CEOs.

APGC is a non-profit and non-political organization, representing the interests of developers and operators of independent energy facilities and independent power marketers. As an association for power generation companies, their coverage encompasses generating capacity from thermal, hydro and other sources, which includes renewable products derived from biomass, geothermal, small hydro, solar, and wind, producers of highly efficient cogeneration facilities.
